perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue until the twentieth (20th) anniversary of the Effective Date; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue indefinitely; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its sole discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 30 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 3.1 creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the agreement's term was defined with a concrete end date, the twentieth anniversary of the Effective Date. However, the insertion of 'shall continue indefinitely' removes this certainty and directly conflicts with the previous statement that the agreement will last for a set duration. Also, there is a change in the owner termination notice from 120 to 30 days, creating also in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue indefinitely; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its sole discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 30 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.1 introduces two direct contradictions. First, it changes the term from a fixed 20-year period to 'indefinitely,' which conflicts with the original specified duration, creating ambiguity and inconsistency in the agreement's term. Second, it changes the notice period for Owner termination from 120 days to 30 days, thus creating conflicting terms within the same section. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction and Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.7 Emergencies. Notwithstanding any provision of this Agreement, in the event of an Emergency, Operator shall proceed with maintenance or repair work or with any other action when necessary to minimize damage and to end the Emergency ("Emergency Work"), without regard to the limits set forth in this Article IV. Operator shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, notify Owner of the existence or occurrence of the Emergency, setting forth the nature of the emergency, the corrective action taken or proposed to be taken, and the actual or estimated cost of such corrective action. Emergency Work shall include only such work as is necessary to immediately address the Emergency and does not include any work necessary to restore the Gathering System or improve the Gathering System in order to permit continued operations.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.7 Emergencies. Notwithstanding any provision of this Agreement, in the event of an Emergency, Operator shall proceed with maintenance or repair work or with any other action when necessary to minimize damage and to end the Emergency ("Emergency Work"), without regard to the limits set forth in this Article IV. Operator shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, notify Owner of the existence or occurrence of the Emergency, setting forth the nature of the emergency, the corrective action taken or proposed to be taken, and the actual or estimated cost of such corrective action. Emergency Work shall include any work necessary to immediately address the Emergency including all work necessary to restore the Gathering System or improve the Gathering System in order to permit continued operations.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 4.1.7 alters the scope of 'Emergency Work'. The original text strictly limited emergency work to immediate actions for damage control, excluding restoration or improvement of the Gathering System. The modified version broadens the definition to include restoration and improvement activities. This insertion creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity about the Operator's responsibility during emergencies. Does it only have to address the emergency at hand or it has to improve the Gathering System?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.7 Emergencies. Notwithstanding any provision of this Agreement, in the event of an Emergency, Operator shall proceed with maintenance or repair work or with any other action when necessary to minimize damage and to end the Emergency ("Emergency Work"), without regard to the limits set forth in this Article IV. Operator shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, notify Owner of the existence or occurrence of the Emergency, setting forth the nature of the emergency, the corrective action taken or proposed to be taken, and the actual or estimated cost of such corrective action. Emergency Work shall include any work necessary to immediately address the Emergency including all work necessary to restore the Gathering System or improve the Gathering System in order to permit continued operations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the scope of 'Emergency Work'. The original text limited emergency work to immediate actions, excluding restoration or improvement. The modified version includes restoration and improvement, directly contradicting the initial limitation. This introduces a clear ambiguity regarding the Operator's responsibilities during emergencies, making enforcement unclear and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale or conveyance of all or any part of the Gathering System. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 9.13 shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.13 Assignment. Operator shall make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder freely without any consent of Owner, which consent shall not be required. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale or conveyance of all or any part of the Gathering System. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 9.13 shall be null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 9.13 modifies the assignment clause, specifically regarding the Operator's ability to assign the agreement. The original text stated that Operator could only assign the agreement with the Owner's written consent, which 'shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed.' The modified version states that Operator can 'make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder freely without any consent of Owner, which consent shall not be required'. The modified language removes the requirement for Owner consent and grants Operator the freedom to assign the agreement without restriction, which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.13 Assignment. Operator shall make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder freely without any consent of Owner, which consent shall not be required. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale or conveyance of all or any part of the Gathering System. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 9.13 shall be null and void.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.13 states that the Operator can make assignments "freely without any consent of Owner, which consent shall not be required." The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) contains the following contradictory clause: "Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 9.13 shall be null and void." This creates an internal contradiction within the same section because it first grants the Operator the right to assign the agreement freely, and then renders any assignment in violation of the *very same section* as null and void. If the operator is free to assign, there would be no potential for violation, thus creating an in-text contradiction as to what an assignment in violation of the section even consists of.
